_comment: Dell BIOS profile. Probed on Dell XPS 15 9570 (PC-DEPLOY, 192.168.5.141) 2026-06-19. CCTK not installed, DCIM_BIOSService not present. DellBIOSProvider (PSGallery) is recommended.

methods_in_priority_order.available_on: All modern Dell systems with BIOS/UEFI
methods_in_priority_order.notes: Values for Set-Item are comma-separated boot device type strings. The exact strings depend on Dell model and firmware.
Exact device names (NIC, HDD, etc.) depend on model. Run 'cctk.exe --BootOrder' first to see available device names.

methods_in_priority_order.description: Universal UEFI EFI variable manipulation — see generic-uefi.json
methods_in_priority_order.notes: Works on any UEFI system but requires SeSystemEnvironmentPrivilege.

wmi_status.DCIM_BIOSService: NOT PRESENT on XPS 15 9570 — may be present on enterprise (Latitude/OptiPlex) models
wmi_status.notes: root\dcim\sysman accessible but no boot-related WMI classes found on XPS consumer hardware.

powershell_probe.check_cctk: Get-ChildItem 'C:\Program Files\Dell' -Filter cctk.exe -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select -First 1 -ExpandProperty FullName
